english: pa (pa) (Lex. w.e.) may accordingly imply: to introduce a custom.

(rtá-po (rta po) C., Mil.), resp. čibs (chibs), 1. horse, ṗo-rta (pho rta) a gelding, mó-rta (mo rta), or rta-rgód-ma (rta rgod ma), a mare; rta _dúl-ba (rta 'dul ba) to break in, train, a horse; rta rgyúg-pa (rta rgyug pa) to gallop; to run horses for a wager, to race Glr.; *sta srul-če* (sta srul che) Ld. id.? - 2. the lower front part of a pair of breeches, dór-rta (dor rta), áṅ-rta (ang rta). Comp. rta-rkyá(-pa) (rta rkya(-pa)), or -skyá(-pa) (-skya(-pa)) one skilled in horsemanship. - rta-bskrágs (rta bskrags) (*stab-rágs* (stab rags) Ld.) a clattering train of horsemen. - rta-gál (rta gal) Ts. pouch or bag of a horseman, saddle-bag. - rta-grás (rta gras) = rta-rá (rta ra). - rta-bgád (rta bgad) a horse-laugh, rta-bgád-kyis _débs-pa (rta bgad kyis 'debs pa) to set up a horse-laugh Sch. -- rta-mgó (rta mgo) a horse's head; rta-mgó-ma (rta mgo ma) v. go-tsém (go tsem). - rta-mgrin (rta mgrin) (हयग्रीव) n. of a demon (Schl. 110), a terrifying deity. - rta-sga (rta sga), W. *té-ga* (te ga), saddle. - rta-sgám (rta sgam) a large box or chest. - rta-sgo (rta sgo) v. sgo (sgo). - rta-sgyél (rta sgyel), gen. connected with mi-bsád (mi bsad), the slaughtering of men and killing of horses. - rta-ṅáṅ (rta ngang) Tibetan horses, small, strong, unshod, v. Hook. II, 131, and so already in Marco Polo's travels. - rta-rṅa (rta rnga) horse-tail, *te ṅá-ma yod* (te nga ma yod) W. it is (made) of horse-hair. - rta-lčág (rta lcag) horse-whip; whip in general - rta-čág (rta chag) dry fodder or provender given to horses, corn, oats. - rta-mčóg (rta mchog) the best horse, a splendid horse, state-horse; gen. a fabulous horse, a sort of Pegasus, thus e.g. Glr. chp. 6, where it partakes of divine properties (rtai rgyál-po čaṅ-šés bá-la-ha (rta'i rgyal po cang shes ba la ha); acc. to Schl. p. 253 rlúṅ-rta (rlung rta) is the same). - rta-mčóg-ḱa- _bab (rta mchog kha 'bab) = yyas-ru-ytsáṅ-po (g.yas ru gtsang po) = mṅá-ris-ču (mnga' ris chu) n. of the principal river of Tibet. - rta-ljáṅ (rta ljang) he with the green horses, the sun, po. Glr. - rta-rná (rta rna) horse-ear, n. of one of the seven gold-mountains, surrounding the Rirab. - rtá-pa (rta pa) horseman, rider, *tá-pa ta-żóṅ* (ta pa ta zhon) Ld. a balancing-board, see-saw; rtá-pai dpúṅ (rta pa'i dpung) horse, cavalry Cs. - rta-lpágs (rta lpags) a horse's skin; n. of a medicinal herb Med. - rta-bábs (rta babs) 1. a large stone or raised place for alighting from a horse(?) Cs. 2. the superstructure of a large door or gate, the arch of a gate-way, Lex. twa-ra-ṇa, तोरण? - rta-dbyáṅs (rta dbyangs) अश्वघोष n. of a great scholar Thgy. - rta-bál (rta bal) a horse's front-hair Cs. - rta-sbáṅs (rta sbangs) horse-dung. - rta-rmíg (rta rmig) a horse's hoof; n. of a plant Med. - rta-rmíg-ma (rta rmig ma) a lump of silver bullion like a horse's hoof Cs. - rta-rdzi (rta rdzi) one that tends horses; a groom Glr. - rta-żóṅ (rta zhon) a good horse. - rta-zám (rta zam) 1. post-station, rta-zám-gyi tsúgs-pa (rta zam gyi tshugs pa) a post-house; rta-zám-gyi spyi-dpón (rta zam gyi spyi dpon) postmaster-general Cs. 2. in Ld. also for rta-zám-pa (rta zam pa). - rta-zám-pa (rta zam pa) postillion, courier, express, estafet. An estafet rides day and night, mounting fresh horses at certain stations, and making the way from Lé to Lhasa (for ordinary travellers a journey of 4 months) in 18 days. - rta(i)- _ú-lag (rta(i) 'u lag) a compulsory service consisting in the supply of horses. - rta-rá (rta ra), rta-grás (rta gras) inclosure, stable, for horses. - rta-šá (rta sha) 1. horse-flesh. 2. the oblique abdominal muscles of the hips. - rta-šád (rta shad) curry-comb Sch. - rta-ysár (rta gsar) a horse not yet broken in or dressed Schr. - rta-gséb (rta gseb) stallion. - With regard to the colour of horses (spú-ḱa (spu kha)), the following distinctions are made: rta-dkár (rta dkar) a gray or white horse; rta-rkyaṅ-nág (rta rkyang nag), or kam-nág (kam nag) Sch. a dark-brown horse; rta kám-pa (rta kam pa) Ld. a yellowish-brown horse (Sch. a dark-brown horse); rta-kam-dmár (rta kam dmar) Sch. a light-bay horse, a sorrel horse; rta krá-bo (rta khra bo) a piebald or a dappled horse Ld. - Glr., Schl. fol. 26, a; rta-gró (rta gro) Sch. a gray horse, rta gro-dkár (rta gro dkar) a light-gray horse, rta gro-sṅón (rta gro sngon) Sch. a dapple-gray horse, rta gro-dmár (rta gro dmar) a roan horse, a roan; rta rgya-bo (rta rgya bo) Sch. a chestnut-bay horse (a bayard, a brown horse) with white breast and muzzle; rta ṅáṅ-pa (rta ngang pa) an isabel Ld. - Glr.; rta rṅog-dkár (rta rngog dkar) a bright bay horse; rta-sṅo-kra (rta sngo khra), rta-sṅo-tig-čaṅ (rta sngo tig cang) Sch. a dapple-gray horse; rta-sṅo-nag (rta sngo nag) Sch. a dark-gray horse; rta-tíg-kru (rta tig khru) Sch. a spotted horse; rta nag (rta nag) a black horse; rta-brau (rta bra'u) = rgya (rgya)
